tasks.name: Create AssessmentData type in dashboard-types.ts
tasks.purpose: Define TypeScript interface for assessment widget data including category_scores, overall_score, highest/lowest categories, and completion status

dependencies: survey_responses table schema with category_scores JSONB
constraints: Interface must match JSONB structure from survey_responses.category_scores
Include fields: category_scores (Record<string, number>), overall_score (number), highest_category (string), lowest_category (string), completed (boolean)
Use TypeScript interface (not type)
Add to existing DashboardData aggregate interface

tasks.input_state: dashboard-types.ts exists with DashboardData interface from S1864.I1.F1. survey_responses table has category_scores JSONB field.
tasks.output_state: AssessmentData interface added to dashboard-types.ts with all required fields. DashboardData.assessment field updated to use AssessmentData | null type.
tasks.acceptance_criterion: AssessmentData interface exists with category_scores, overall_score, highest_category, lowest_category, completed fields. TypeScript compiles without errors.

tasks.name: Add loadAssessment function to dashboard-page.loader.ts
tasks.purpose: Implement loader function that fetches survey_responses.category_scores for the authenticated user and transforms JSONB to AssessmentData format

constraints: Query survey_responses where user_id = auth.uid() and completed = true
Return null if no completed assessment exists
Transform category_scores JSONB to Record<string, number>
Calculate overall_score as average of category scores
Use RLS-protected client (not admin client)
Add error logging with getLogger()

tasks.input_state: dashboard-page.loader.ts has placeholder loadAssessment function returning null. AssessmentData type defined.
tasks.output_state: loadAssessment function queries survey_responses, transforms JSONB category_scores, calculates overall score, returns AssessmentData or null.
tasks.acceptance_criterion: loadAssessment function fetches data from survey_responses, transforms category_scores JSONB correctly, calculates overall score, handles empty state (returns null), includes error logging.
tasks.verification_command: grep -q 'async function loadAssessment' apps/web/app/home/\(user\)/_lib/server/dashboard-page.loader.ts && grep -q 'survey_responses' apps/web/app/home/\(user\)/_lib/server/dashboard-page.loader.ts && pnpm typecheck

tasks.name: Create transformCategoryScoresToRadarData helper function
tasks.purpose: Create pure function that transforms category_scores Record<string, number> to Recharts RadarChart array format [{category: string, score: number}]

constraints: Input: Record<string, number> (JSONB from database)
Output: Array<{category: string, score: number}>
Function is pure (no side effects)
Export as named export
Add JSDoc comment with example

tasks.input_state: No transformer function exists. AssessmentData type defines category_scores as Record<string, number>.
tasks.output_state: transformCategoryScoresToRadarData function exported from assessment-spider-widget.tsx, converts Record to Array format for RadarChart.
tasks.acceptance_criterion: Function exists, takes Record<string, number>, returns Array<{category: string, score: number}>, TypeScript compiles, includes JSDoc example.

tasks.name: Create AssessmentSpiderWidgetSkeleton component
tasks.purpose: Create loading skeleton component matching the spider chart widget dimensions (aspect-square) for Suspense fallback

constraints: Use aspect-square for chart skeleton (matches RadarChart aspect ratio)
Include skeleton for title (h2 width)
Include skeleton for overall score badge
Include skeleton for View Assessment button
Export as default export

tasks.input_state: No skeleton component exists. Skeleton primitive available from @kit/ui/skeleton.
tasks.output_state: File apps/web/app/home/(user)/_components/assessment-spider-widget-skeleton.tsx exists with Card layout and skeleton placeholders for chart, title, score, button.
tasks.acceptance_criterion: Skeleton component exists at correct path, uses Card layout, includes aspect-square chart skeleton, title skeleton, score skeleton, button skeleton, default exported.

tasks.name: Create AssessmentSpiderWidget component with populated state

action.target: spider chart widget component with data display

tasks.purpose: Create Client Component that renders RadarChart with category scores, overall score badge, highest/lowest category indicators, and View Assessment link

dependencies: RadarChart, Radar, PolarGrid, PolarAngleAxis from recharts
constraints: Must be Client Component (use client directive)
Use ResponsiveContainer with initialDimension for SSR safety
Display overall score prominently (e.g., 'Overall: 72%')
Use Badge to highlight highest/lowest categories
Link to /home/(user)/assessment/survey for View Assessment
Use aspect-square for chart container
Follow existing radar-chart.tsx pattern for Recharts setup

tasks.input_state: AssessmentData type defined. transformCategoryScoresToRadarData function exists. radar-chart.tsx provides reference implementation.
tasks.output_state: AssessmentSpiderWidget component renders RadarChart with category scores, displays overall score badge, highlights highest/lowest categories, includes View Assessment link.
tasks.acceptance_criterion: Component is Client Component, renders RadarChart with PolarGrid/PolarAngleAxis/Radar, displays overall score, shows highest/lowest category badges, includes View Assessment link, uses ChartContainer wrapper, TypeScript compiles.
tasks.verification_command: grep -q '"use client"' apps/web/app/home/\(user\)/_components/assessment-spider-widget.tsx && grep -q 'RadarChart' apps/web/app/home/\(user\)/_components/assessment-spider-widget.tsx && grep -q 'ChartContainer' apps/web/app/home/\(user\)/_components/assessment-spider-widget.tsx && pnpm typecheck

tasks.name: Add empty state to AssessmentSpiderWidget

action.target: empty state UI when no assessment data

tasks.purpose: Add conditional rendering to show 'Complete Assessment' CTA with EmptyState component when assessment data is null

constraints: Show when data prop is null
Use EmptyState component with title and description
Include 'Complete Assessment' button linking to /home/(user)/assessment/survey
Title: 'No Assessment Data'
Description: 'Complete the presentation skills assessment to see your scores.'

tasks.input_state: AssessmentSpiderWidget component exists with populated state only. EmptyState component available.
tasks.output_state: AssessmentSpiderWidget checks if data is null, renders EmptyState with 'Complete Assessment' CTA when null, otherwise shows RadarChart.
tasks.acceptance_criterion: Component conditionally renders EmptyState when data is null, EmptyState has correct title/description, includes button linking to assessment survey, TypeScript compiles.

tasks.name: Integrate AssessmentSpiderWidget into dashboard page

action.target: AssessmentSpiderWidget to dashboard page with Suspense

tasks.purpose: Import and render AssessmentSpiderWidget in dashboard page.tsx grid with skeleton fallback, passing assessment data from loadDashboardPageData

constraints: Wrap widget in Suspense with skeleton fallback
Place in grid position 2 (row 1, col 2 per spec layout)
Pass data.assessment prop to widget
Import both widget and skeleton components

tasks.input_state: Dashboard page.tsx exists with grid layout from S1864.I1.F2. AssessmentSpiderWidget and skeleton components complete.
tasks.output_state: page.tsx imports AssessmentSpiderWidget and skeleton, renders widget in grid position 2 wrapped in Suspense, passes assessment data prop.
tasks.acceptance_criterion: Widget imported and rendered in page.tsx, wrapped in Suspense with skeleton fallback, placed in correct grid position (row 1, col 2), receives assessment data from loader, TypeScript compiles.
tasks.verification_command: grep -q 'AssessmentSpiderWidget' apps/web/app/home/\(user\)/page.tsx && grep -q 'Suspense' apps/web/app/home/\(user\)/page.tsx && grep -q 'assessment-spider-widget-skeleton' apps/web/app/home/\(user\)/page.tsx && pnpm typecheck

groups.name: Widget Implementation - Populated and Empty States
